Package com.runticket.model

package com.runticket.model
Models for the RunTicket application. These classes represent the core data structures such as Customer, Event, Venue, and Information. They encapsulate the attributes and behaviors relevant to each entity, such as managing event details, customer information, and venue specifics.
Author:
Francisco Garcia, Adrian Sifuentes, Henry Ng, Sebastian Marquez
  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    com.runticket
    RunTicket Application Includes models, views, controllers, and services for managing ticket sales.
    com.runticket.controller
    Set of controllers for the RunTicket application.
    com.runticket.service
    Services for the RunTicket application.
    com.runticket.view
    Views for the RunTicket application.
  • Class
    Description
    Arena
    The Arena class represents an arena venue with a name, type, and capacity.
    Auditorium
    The Auditorium class represents an auditorium venue with a name, type, and capacity.
    Concert
    The Concert class represents a concert event and extends the Event class.
    Customer
    The Customer class represents a customer with personal details, membership status, balance, and a list of invoices for purchased tickets.
    Event
    The Event class represents an event with details such as name, date, time, type, cost, venue, tickets, seating, and invoices.
    Festival
    The Festival class represents a festival event and extends the Event class.
    Field
    The Field class represents a field venue with a name, type, and capacity.
    Information
    Singleton class that holds the main data structures for events, customers, and venues.
    Invoice
    The Invoice class represents an invoice for a ticket purchase by a Customer for a specific event.
    OpenAir
    The OpenAir class represents an open air venue with a name, type, and capacity.
    Outdoor
    The Outdoor class represents an outdoor venue with a name, type, and capacity.
    Sport
    The Sport class represents a sport event, inheriting from the Event class.
    Stadium
    The Stadium class represents a stadium venue with a name, type, and capacity.
    Tickets
    The Tickets class represents a ticket with a tier, price, quantity, and percentage available.
    Venue
    The Venue interface defines the methods that any venue type must implement.
